title: Create Documentation Page Component
description: Develop a new documentation page component within the React frontend scaffold to serve as the main docs page for the project.
details: Add a new page under the appropriate routing (e.g., /docs) in the React frontend (apps/web). The component should follow the project's folder and code style conventions. Use TypeScript and ensure the component is modular and easy to extend. Include a clear page title and structure for documentation content.
test_strategy: Verify the /docs route renders the new documentation page without errors. Check for correct page title and initial structure. Use Jest and React Testing Library to ensure the component renders as expected.

title: Populate Documentation Content
description: Write and organize comprehensive documentation content covering project setup, structure, usage, and extension guidelines.
details: Populate the docs page with well-structured sections: Overview, Getting Started, Project Structure, Development Workflow, Authentication & AI Modules, Infrastructure, and Contribution Guidelines. Use headings, lists, and code snippets for clarity. Reference content from the PRD and README as appropriate.
test_strategy: Manually review the rendered documentation for completeness, clarity, and formatting. Ensure all sections are present and content is accurate. Optionally, write snapshot tests to verify content rendering.

title: Add Navigation Link to Docs Page
description: Integrate a navigation link to the new documentation page in the main site navigation.
details: Update the main navigation component (e.g., Navbar) to include a link to /docs. Ensure the link is styled consistently with other navigation items and is accessible from all pages.
test_strategy: Check that the Docs link appears in the navigation and routes correctly to the documentation page. Use React Testing Library to simulate navigation and assert correct behavior.

title: Implement Basic Styling and Responsiveness
description: Apply styling to the documentation page to ensure readability and responsiveness across devices.
details: Use the project's existing CSS or styling solution to format the documentation page. Ensure headings, lists, and code blocks are visually distinct. Test layout on desktop and mobile viewports. Follow accessibility best practices for color contrast and font sizes.
test_strategy: Manually test the documentation page on different screen sizes. Use browser dev tools to check responsiveness. Optionally, add visual regression tests if supported.
